reasoning: With only ~4.5 months remaining in 2025, Elon Musk leaving his role as CEO of Tesla is highly unlikely. While there were significant rumors and investor pressure in early/mid-2025 regarding a potential departure due to his distraction managing the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), Musk officially officially stepped down from that government role in late May 2025 to refocus on his businesses. At the same time, both Musk and the Board explicitly denied April WSJ reports of succession planning, with Musk publicly declaring he intends to remain CEO for at least five more years (and adding that only 'death' would make him step down). Furthermore, on August 4, 2025, Tesla granted Musk a new $29 billion stock compensation package to replace his rescinded 2018 package. This recent massive financial recommitment practically ensures neither he nor the board plan a transition this year. Thus, any departure before the end of 2025 would likely have to stem from an extreme 'black swan' event like sudden death, severe incapacitation, or a massive unforeseen scandal, which collectively carry a very low probability (~3-4%) over such a short time horizon.
